title: trifolium parryi
intro: Trifolium parryi, also known as Parry's clover, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its white or pink flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Trifolium parryi is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Parry's clover include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
